mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5 percent, or signal loses predictive power with p-value above 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity that supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 205.0, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict positive returns, or the interaction term loses significance with p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential investment, which reduces construction sector revenues and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: HOUST declines do not precede negative 60-day returns in late-cycle regimes, or the predictive relationship reverses sign in walk-forward validation.
